# Query Context

The query context layer decides what the model sees before any user turn is sent.

## System Prompt Assembly

`utils/queryContext.ts` builds the cached prefix for a query by gathering three pieces:
- the default system prompt
- the user context
- the system context

If a custom system prompt is supplied, the default prompt and system context are intentionally skipped so the replacement prompt stays clean.

## User Context

`context.ts` builds user context from data that should be available for the whole session, such as:
- `CLAUDE.md` content discovered from the workspace
- the current date
- optional coordinator-specific context

This is where project guidance gets injected into the model's view of the session.

## System Context

The system context carries information such as git status and cache-breaker injection. It is computed once per conversation and cached for the session.

## Side-Question Fallback

`buildSideQuestionFallbackParams()` reconstructs a safe cache prefix for resume-time side questions. It exists so the SDK path can answer a side question even before the full turn machinery has stored a cache-safe snapshot.

## Why the Cache Prefix Matters

The agent is tuned to keep prompt assembly stable enough to preserve cache hits across turns. That means the context layer is not just informational; it is a performance boundary.
